title: Channel
description: A Slack conversation object representing a channel, direct message, multi-person DM, or group conversation.

id.description: The unique identifier for this conversation.

name.description: The name of the channel (without the leading # sign).

name_normalized.description: Normalized version of the channel name.

is_channel.description: Whether this is a public channel.

is_group.description: Whether this is a private channel (formerly a group).

is_im.description: Whether this is a direct message.

is_mpim.description: Whether this is a multi-person direct message.

is_private.description: Whether this is a private conversation.

is_shared.description: Whether the channel is shared with another workspace.

is_ext_shared.description: Whether the channel is shared with an external organization via Slack Connect.

is_org_shared.description: Whether the channel is shared across an Enterprise Grid org.

is_pending_ext_shared.description: Whether there is a pending external share invitation.

is_member.description: Whether the calling user is a member of this conversation.

created.description: Unix timestamp of when the conversation was created.

creator.description: User ID of the member who created this channel.

updated.description: Unix timestamp of the last update to the conversation.

unlinked.description: Number of times the channel has been unlinked.

previous_names.description: A list of previous names the channel had.

num_members.description: The number of members in the channel. Only included when specifically requested.

locale.description: IETF language code for the channel locale. Only included when specifically requested.

user.description: For direct messages, the user ID of the other participant.

shared_team_ids.description: Array of team IDs that share this channel (for shared channels).

parent_conversation.description: Parent conversation ID for thread-only channels.
